title: S7: bee knowledge promote — finished work PROPOSES knowledge, never writes truth silently
action: Per D38 + plan.md S7 (advisor rated this HIGH risk: proposals only). Add `promote` to knowledge.mjs. `bee knowledge promote --work <id> [--json]` gathers and SHAPES, never commits truth: (1) read the bee.work-item concept for <id>; (2) read that feature's CAPPED cells from .bee/cells/*.json (a READ of the runtime store — permitted by D2; never a write) — their outcome, files_changed, deviations, verification_evidence, behavior_change flag; (3) emit a PROPOSAL document (stdout, and --json as structured data) with three sections: (a) DELIVERY DRAFT — a complete bee.delivery concept in canonical emitter form (what shipped, how verified with the recorded verify evidence, deviations) ready to be saved as work/<id>/delivery.md; (b) AREA UPDATES — for each area named in the work item's bee.areas, the capped behavior_change cells whose files_changed touch that area's subject, as candidate spec-sync bullets, each citing its cell id (a PROPOSAL of what to add, never an edit); (c) PATTERN CANDIDATES — cells whose trace carries deviations or a failure_signature, shaped as candidate bee.pattern concepts with bee.polarity pitfall, each traceable to its cell. NOTHING is written into docs/knowledge/ by promote — it prints/returns. The apply step is a human or agent decision, and the profile spec must say so. Unknown work id = typed error exit 1. Register knowledge.promote (runnable example); wire HANDLERS. Document promote in docs/specs/okf-profile.md (Behaviors + a Business Rule: promote proposes, never writes). RED-FIRST: extend test_knowledge.mjs — fixture bundle + fixture cells dir: proposal names the capped cells, delivery draft is canonical (round-trips through check), a cell with deviations yields a pattern candidate, a clean cell yields none, promote writes ZERO files (assert the bundle is byte-identical before/after), unknown id exits 1. Propagation sequence LAST; ONE commit.

truths: red evidence: new tests failed before implementation (verbatim)
promote writes ZERO files — proven by a byte-identical bundle snapshot before/after a real run (evidence in trace)

DOGFOOD: `bee knowledge promote --work okf-foundation` on this repo names the real capped cells okf-1..okf-8 and produces a delivery draft that passes check when saved to a temp bundle file (then removed) — full output quoted in the trace
unknown --work id exits 1 with a typed error
prohibitions: promote NEVER writes into docs/knowledge/ or anywhere else (D2, proposals only)
promote NEVER writes .bee/*.json(l) — cells are read-only input
no invented content in proposals: every proposed line traces to a cell trace or the work item

trace.outcome: bee knowledge promote shipped (D38): finished work PROPOSES a delivery draft, area spec-sync bullets and pitfall candidates from capped cell traces, and writes nothing — proven by a 498-file byte-identical snapshot of docs/knowledge/ + .bee/cells/ across a real run
deviations: Edited skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs (declared in the cell's files list) to add the knowledge.promote registry-example execution: that suite's 'every registry entry had its example executed at least once' bijection fails the moment a new registry entry lands without one. The example runs against the isolated rootKnowledge fixture repo, seeded with one capped cell trace, and asserts promote left both the fixture bundle file and the fixture cell trace byte-identical.

The cell's zero-write must-have was written as 'a byte-identical bundle snapshot before/after a real run'. The first version of the suite check snapshotted the ENTIRE fixture repo across a CLI run and went red on .bee/cache/manifest-hash.json — which promote does not write: the dispatcher writes it on every bee invocation. Proven, not assumed: a read-only 'knowledge check' in a virgin fixture repo produces exactly the same one-file delta. The check was therefore split into an in-process proof (buildPromotion, whole tree, zero delta of any kind) plus a CLI proof scoped to the bundle and the runtime store, with the dispatcher-cache delta asserted equal to check's. The real-repo 498-file sha256 proof over docs/knowledge/ + .bee/cells/ is byte-clean.

The okf-foundation work item declares no bee.areas, so the dogfood's AREA UPDATES section is legitimately empty. Implemented exactly as specified (areas come from the work item, never guessed — D10) and recorded as an Open Gap in docs/specs/okf-profile.md; the populated path is covered by fixtures in both test_knowledge.mjs and test_bee_cli.mjs.

  "verify_command": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs",
  "verify_tail": "PASS run_verify: 65 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=58084ms — incl. 'PASS skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_knowledge.mjs' (53 checks, 10 of them the new promote suite), 'PASS skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs' (239 checks incl. the knowledge.promote registry-example execution and the every-entry-example-executed bijection), 'PASS scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs', 'PASS scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check', 'PASS scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check' (434 files), 'PASS skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_plugin_distribution.mjs', 'PASS .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge check', 'PASS .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge index --check'",
  "red_failure_evidence": "RED-FIRST: the 10 promote checks were appended to skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_knowledge.mjs and run BEFORE any implementation. Verbatim run (.bee/tmp/okf-9/red.txt): 'FAIL  CLI: knowledge promote --json names exactly the CAPPED cells of the work item feature, in id order (D38)' / 'FAIL  knowledge promote: the DELIVERY DRAFT is a complete canonical bee.delivery concept that round-trips through check (zero not_canonical)' / 'FAIL  knowledge promote: a cell with a deviation or a failure signature yields a pitfall candidate; a clean cell yields none (D18 polarity)' / 'FAIL  knowledge promote: AREA UPDATES list the behavior_change cells whose files touch that area subject, each citing its cell id' / 'FAIL  knowledge promote WRITES NOTHING: the whole repo tree is byte-identical before and after a real run (D2)' / 'FAIL  knowledge promote: the human form carries the three proposal sections and says nothing was written' / 'FAIL  knowledge promote: an unknown --work id exits 1 with a typed error (D38)' / 'FAIL  knowledge promote: a work item with no capped cells still proposes a canonical (empty-evidence) draft, never a throw' / 'FAIL  CLI: knowledge.promote appears in the bee --help --json manifest with a runnable example (test_bee_cli conformance)' — summary line '43 passed, 9 failed'. The dispatcher's own refusal was quoted in the failures: 'Unknown command \"promote\". Use: check, index, list, context.' After implementation the same file reports '53 passed, 0 failed'.",
  "zero_write_proof": "TWO independent proofs. (1) REAL REPO, byte-identical snapshot: 'find docs/knowledge .bee/cells -type f | sort | xargs sha256sum' captured 498 files before and after the real run 'node .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge promote --work okf-foundation'; 'diff snap-before.txt snap-after.txt' produced ZERO output — 498/498 files byte-identical, nothing created, nothing deleted, nothing modified. (2) SUITE, two layers: 'knowledge promote WRITES NOTHING: buildPromotion leaves the whole repo tree byte-identical, in-process (D2)' snapshots every file of a fixture repo as hex bytes, calls buildPromotion() directly (no dispatcher in the picture) and asserts the file SET and every file's bytes are unchanged; 'knowledge promote WRITES NOTHING through the CLI either' does the same across two CLI runs (--json and human) over docs/knowledge/ + .bee/cells/ + .bee/onboarding.json, then proves the one remaining delta (.bee/cache/manifest-hash.json) is the DISPATCHER's, not promote's, by showing a read-only 'knowledge check' in a virgin repo produces exactly the same delta. Structurally: buildPromotion/readCappedCellTraces contain no fs.writeFileSync/mkdirSync/rmSync call, handleKnowledgePromote only prints, there is no --apply flag, and the payload carries writes: [] as the machine-readable contract.",
  "dogfood": "node .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge promote --work okf-foundation on THIS repo resolved work/okf-foundation/work-item.md and mined all 8 real capped cells: 'promote proposal for work item \"okf-foundation\" (work/okf-foundation/work-item.md) — 8 capped cell(s): okf-1, okf-2, okf-3, okf-4, okf-5, okf-6, okf-7, okf-8'. (a) DELIVERY DRAFT — 6721-byte canonical bee.delivery concept for docs/knowledge/work/okf-foundation/delivery.md: bee.id okf-foundation-delivery, tags/decisions/lane carried from the work item, bee.sources citing the work item plus all 8 .bee/cells/okf-N.json traces, a 'What shipped' bullet per cell quoting its recorded outcome, a 'Verify' bullet per cell quoting its recorded verify command + evidence, and okf-1's one recorded deviation. (b) AREA UPDATES — 'None: the work item declares no bee.areas, so there is no area to sync (D19)' (honest empty section; recorded as an Open Gap in the profile spec). (c) PATTERN CANDIDATES — 1: okf-foundation-okf-1-pitfall (bee.polarity pitfall, bee.lifecycle draft, sources [.bee/cells/okf-1.json]) quoting okf-1's deviation verbatim. Footer: 'knowledge promote: 8 capped cell(s) mined, 1 delivery draft, 0 area bullet(s), 1 pattern candidate(s), 0 file(s) written.' Full output captured in .bee/tmp/okf-9/dogfood.txt and quoted in the worker report.",
  "delivery_draft_round_trip": "The dogfood draft was saved into a COPY of the real bundle under .bee/tmp/okf-9/draftcheck/ (the real docs/knowledge/ was never written to) and graded: 'bee knowledge check --json' returned okf errors [] , profile warnings [] , counts {files: 61, concepts: 54, errors: 0, warnings: 0} — zero OKF errors and zero profile warnings, including ZERO not_canonical, so the emitted draft is already canonical emitter form. Sandbox removed afterwards.",
  "unknown_work": "'bee knowledge promote --work no-such-work --json' exits 1 with {\"error\":\"knowledge promote: unknown_work — no bee.work-item concept in docs/knowledge/ carries bee.id \\\"no-such-work\\\" (D38).\"} on stdout; the human form exits 1 with the same typed message on stderr. Pinned by the suite.",
  "scope_discipline": "No --strict anywhere (registry example is 'bee knowledge promote --work okf-foundation --json'; the suite asserts every knowledge example is --strict-free). docs/decisions/index.md untouched (D11). templates/lib is the source of truth — .bee/bin/lib/ and .bee/bin/bee.mjs are byte-copies (test_lib_mirror green). Propagation ran last in order: onboard_bee --apply, render_plugin_skill_trees (127 files x2 trees), release_manifest --write (434 files), then run_verify."
}
